extra_model_fields.notes_for_worker: Prioritize adding the formulas_export and the complete Recommendation section; the computed outputs CSV enables verifiers to fully validate numeric claims even if openpyxl remains unavailable.
verification_checklist_for_revised_attem: Open the XLSX in a standard parser OR confirm formulas_export accurately lists formulas.
Run an independent script that ingests computed_outputs.csv and verifies weighted_score, rank, and sensitivity deltas match the chemistry_note.
Confirm Recommendation table totals and mapping demonstrate budget fit and label smallest-defensible intervention.
Ensure no duplicated/conflicting metadata fields remain in the grader/verifier outputs.

planned_changes.description: Generate formulas_export.txt (or CSV) enumerating sheet!cell -> formula for Inputs, Scoring, Sensitivity, and Validation sheets. Include a one-line checksum or hash to tie the export to the XLSX.

Replace chemistry_note.md with a non-truncated version that contains a Recommendation section including: intervention table (unit cost, quantity, total), explicit mapping from intervention lines to record IDs, and calculation showing the chosen intervention is the smallest defensible option within decision_budget_usd=182500.

Add two worked recalculation examples showing intermediate arithmetic for +5% shifts (one for the dominant metric intermediate_stability and one for confidence or barrier) and show delta computations.

Provide a computed_outputs.csv or .json containing for each record: raw inputs, normalized metrics, weighted_score, rank, top_quartile, needs_review, actionability, and sensitivity deltas so automated graders can re-run numeric checks without reading formulas.
Remove duplicated/contradictory grader metadata blocks before publishing; ensure passed_criteria/failed_criteria are presented once and consistently.

If possible, re-generate the XLSX using an approach that preserves explicit formulas and confirm with a CI step that a standard library (openpyxl or an alternate) can open it; if environment constraints persist, document that and ensure formula export + computed outputs cover verifiability needs.

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Improve verifiability and complete Recommendation / cost-accounting details so ri_4 can be fully validated and remove metadata inconsistencies; provide machine-readable formula and numeric exports to avoid dependence on openpyxl in the grader environment.

metadata_json.revision_goal: The submission meets the artifact contract and documents methods, per-record scoring, and sensitivity protocol; however automated verification was limited by an environment inability to open the XLSX and by a truncated chemistry_note that omitted the Recommendation / intervention-cost table. The grader and verifier also show a small metadata inconsistency (duplicated/contradictory passed/failed fields). Because cost/accounting evidence is not visible, ri_4 received partial credit.
